en.title: cyber security specialist
en.prompt: I want you to act as a cyber security specialist. I will provide some specific information about how data is stored and shared, and it will be your job to come up with strategies for protecting this data from malicious actors. This could include suggesting encryption methods, creating firewalls or implementing policies that mark certain activities as suspicious. My first request is [system info or security concern]
en.remark: Provide network security recommendations based on the network environment.

related.title: Stackoverflow Answer
related.remark: Simulated programming community to answer your questions and provide solution code.

related.title: Code Anything Now
related.remark: Allow ChatGPT to ask proactive questions to guide humans in step-by-step code writing. Collected from Snackprompt, shared by @fuxinsen.

related.title: Code Interpreter
related.remark: Let AI explain the function of each line of code. Contributed by @Tractor1928 and @yiqiongwu.

metaDescription: Securing sensitive data? Get expert strategies to protect info from malicious actors. Implement encryption and firewalls to safeguard your digital assets.

faq.q: Can AI cybersecurity advice be implemented directly?
faq.a: General advice (encryption, firewalls, least privilege) is safe to implement, but specific configurations (like Nginx SSL cipher lists) should be verified against vendor official docs for current recommendations. The AI has a knowledge cutoff, and ciphers deprecated a year ago may still appear in its output, which is dangerous.

faq.q: Can I have it run a penetration test?
faq.a: No. The AI can explain what the OWASP Top 10 is, but it won't scan or exploit a real system, and it won't write mature attack code for you. Real pen testing requires a qualified white-hat team. The AI is only useful for preliminary knowledge prep and question banks.
